openingQuery: Wrap-ready gifts with no extra effort

chat.content: Wrap-ready gifts with no extra effort

A few gift-table mainstays worth considering: the Beeswax Taper Candles ($48) — wrap-ready, burn for eight hours, no fussy preamble. The Ceramic Ring Dish ($35) if she tends to leave things on her bedside table. And if you want something that arrives with more occasion, the Botanical Silk Scarf ($128) reads as a proper gift without being obvious.

plan.flowSummary: Dispatcher → Curator + the-gift-table → find_pieces_hybrid (vector + Postgres FTS → RRF → cohere.rerank-v3-5:0)

toolCalls.description: Hybrid pgvector + Postgres FTS + cohere.rerank-v3-5:0 over the gift-band catalog
toolCalls.sql:
WITH vector_results AS (
  SELECT product_id, row_number() OVER (ORDER BY embedding <=> $1::vector) AS vector_rank
  FROM product_catalog
  WHERE quantity > 0
  LIMIT 20
),
fts_results AS (
  SELECT product_id, row_number() OVER (ORDER BY ts_rank_cd(description_tsv, $2::tsquery) DESC) AS text_rank
  FROM product_catalog
  WHERE quantity > 0
    AND description_tsv @@ $2::tsquery
  LIMIT 20
),
rrf AS (
  SELECT COALESCE(v.product_id, f.product_id) AS product_id,
         COALESCE(1.0 / (60 + v.vector_rank), 0) +
         COALESCE(1.0 / (60 + f.text_rank), 0) AS rrf_score
  FROM vector_results v
  FULL OUTER JOIN fts_results f USING (product_id)
)
SELECT product_id, rrf_score
FROM rrf
ORDER BY rrf_score DESC
LIMIT 30;
toolCalls.resultSummary: 30-candidate pool reranked to 3 gift-ready pieces, $35–$128 range

subSteps.label: Postgres FTS branch (to_tsquery OR-of-stems via _build_or_tsquery, k=20)

memoryPills.content: Wants wrap-ready gifts that need no extra effort
Prefers items that ship flat and arrive gift-ready
Bought a sundress as a birthday gift last month

telemetry.title: Memory Recall — Semantic
telemetry.description: Loaded Anna's gift-buyer profile from AgentCore Memory: ships-flat, wrap-ready, warm-neutral palette. Used to weight gift-band candidates.

telemetry.title: Memory Recall — Episodic
telemetry.description: Read Aurora episodic memory: last month's birthday-gift sundress purchase reinforces the wrap-ready intent.
telemetry.sql: SELECT event_type, content, ts FROM customer_episodic_seed WHERE customer_id = $1 ORDER BY ts DESC LIMIT 10;

telemetry.title: vector branch
telemetry.description: pgvector cosine over product_catalog (k=20); query embedding via cohere.embed-english-v3
telemetry.sql:
WITH query_embedding AS (
  SELECT $1::vector AS embedding
)
SELECT
  p.product_id,
  p.name,
  p.brand,
  p.price,
  1 - (p.embedding <=> q.embedding) AS similarity
FROM product_catalog p
CROSS JOIN query_embedding q
WHERE p.quantity > 0
ORDER BY p.embedding <=> q.embedding
LIMIT 20;

telemetry.title: Postgres FTS branch
telemetry.description: ts_rank_cd over description_tsv with GIN index (k=20)
telemetry.sql:
WITH query_terms AS (
  SELECT to_tsquery('english', 'wrap:* | gift:* | ready:* | effort:*') AS q
)
SELECT
  p.product_id,
  p.name,
  p.brand,
  p.price,
  ts_rank_cd(p.description_tsv, query_terms.q) AS text_rank
FROM product_catalog p
CROSS JOIN query_terms
WHERE p.quantity > 0
  AND p.description_tsv @@ query_terms.q
ORDER BY text_rank DESC
LIMIT 20;

telemetry.title: RRF merge
telemetry.sql:
WITH vector_results AS (
  SELECT product_id, row_number() OVER (ORDER BY embedding <=> $1::vector) AS vector_rank
  FROM product_catalog
  WHERE quantity > 0
  LIMIT 20
),
fts_results AS (
  SELECT product_id, row_number() OVER (ORDER BY ts_rank_cd(description_tsv, $2::tsquery) DESC) AS text_rank
  FROM product_catalog
  WHERE quantity > 0
    AND description_tsv @@ $2::tsquery
  LIMIT 20
),
rrf AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(v.product_id, f.product_id) AS product_id,
    COALESCE(1.0 / (60 + v.vector_rank), 0) +
    COALESCE(1.0 / (60 + f.text_rank), 0) AS rrf_score
  FROM vector_results v
  FULL OUTER JOIN fts_results f USING (product_id)
)
SELECT product_id, rrf_score
FROM rrf
ORDER BY rrf_score DESC
LIMIT 30;

sections.title: The ask
paragraphs: Anna arrived with a real gift-shopper's constraint: wrap-ready, no extra effort. The Curator, with the-gift-table skill loaded, pulled three pieces across price bands so she could read her way into a choice rather than be handed one.

sections.title: Why hybrid retrieval matters here
paragraphs: 'Wrap-ready gifts with no extra effort' mixes literal packaging language with soft occasion intent. Postgres FTS caught wrap-ready terms the vector branch could blur; pgvector kept adjacent gift-table pieces in the pool. RRF merged the two signals; Cohere Rerank then put the Beeswax Taper Candles near the top because Cohere read 'wrap-ready' and 'no fussy preamble' as the right register for an unsure giver.
